panels.description: Wide shot: A Regency-era ballroom, elegant chandeliers, guests dancing in the background. In the foreground, Elizabeth talks animatedly with Colonel Forster, while Mr. Darcy stands nearby, arms crossed, watching them with a stoic, observant gaze.

bubbles.text: At Sir William Lucas's party, Elizabeth finds her conversation with Colonel Forster under the scrutiny of Mr. Darcy.

panels.description: Medium shot: Elizabeth turns to face Mr. Darcy, her expression defiant and curious. Darcy stands tall, his expression unreadable, meeting her gaze.

bubbles.text: What does Mr. Darcy mean, by listening to my conversation with Colonel Forster?
That is a question which Mr. Darcy only can answer.

panels.description: Medium shot: Elizabeth gestures towards Miss Lucas, teasingly looking at Darcy. Darcy responds with a thin, dry smile.

bubbles.text: Did not you think, Mr. Darcy, that I expressed myself uncommonly well?
With great energy; but it is a subject which always makes a lady energetic.

panels.description: Close up: Elizabeth seated at the piano, her hands poised on the keys, looking back over her shoulder at Darcy with a mischievous glint in her eyes.

bubbles.text: You are a very strange creature... I would rather not sit down before those who must be in the habit of hearing the best performers.

panels.description: Wide shot: The party atmosphere shifts. Elizabeth stands in the center of the room, speaking with animation, while Darcy looms in the background shadows, looking skeptical.

bubbles.text: What a charming amusement! Dancing is the first refinement of polished society.
Certainly, sir; and it has the advantage of being in vogue amongst the less polished societies: every savage can dance.
